findings.issue: Reviewer report paths contain unfilled template placeholder `{round}` — e.g. `logs/working/task-1/code-reviewer-{round}.json`. This is a literal template token, not a real path.
findings.fix: Replace `{round}` with the initial round number: `logs/working/task-1/code-reviewer-1.json`, `logs/working/task-1/security-auditor-1.json`, `logs/working/task-1/test-reviewer-1.json`

findings.issue: Reviewer report paths contain unfilled template placeholder `{round}` — e.g. `logs/working/task-2/code-reviewer-{round}.json`.
Reviewer report paths contain unfilled template placeholder `{round}` — e.g. `logs/working/task-3/code-reviewer-{round}.json`.
Reviewer report paths contain unfilled template placeholder `{round}` — e.g. `logs/working/task-4/code-reviewer-{round}.json`.
Reviewer report paths contain unfilled template placeholder `{round}` — e.g. `logs/working/task-5/code-reviewer-{round}.json`.
`decisions.md` is absent from Context Files. Template marks it as mandatory-always. Other tasks (2, 3, 4, 5) include it.
findings.fix: Add `- [decisions.md](../decisions.md)` to the Context Files list.

findings.issue: `src/proxy_addon.py` and `src/main.py` are listed as plain text, not as markdown links. Template requires all context file entries to be markdown links with both name and path.
`project.md` and `architecture.md` are absent from Context Files. Template marks both as mandatory-always (project context). Every other task in the batch includes them.
`teammate_name` field is absent. Template includes it (even if empty). Tasks 2, 4, 5 have `teammate_name:` (empty value). Task 1 omits the field entirely.
findings.fix: Add `teammate_name:` line to frontmatter (empty value is fine).

findings.issue: Required Skills entry is missing the SKILL.md markdown link. Template format is `/skill:code-writing` — [SKILL.md path](path). Task 1 only has `/skill:code-writing` with no link.
`teammate_name` field is absent. Template includes it (even if empty). Tasks 2, 4, 5 have the field.

Two of three TDD Anchor tests (`test_mixed_case_head`, `test_uppercase_doctype`) guard behavior by verifying a return value (inject position), which is correct, but the description does not clarify what position/value is expected — the description is vague ('returns correct position'). Anchor should state what the expected outcome is.
findings.fix: Extend descriptions to include the expected return value, e.g.: `test_mixed_case_head` — `<Head>` in HTML returns inject position > 0 (inside the tag), same as lowercase `<head>`

findings.issue: Smoke section duplicates the Automated section: both list `python -m pytest test/ -x -v`. The Smoke section is meant for executable non-test-suite checks (curl, python -c, docker build, local server). A full test suite run is not a smoke check.
findings.fix: Replace the Smoke section content with a quick sanity command, e.g. `python -c "from src.proxy_addon import FlowCleanup; print('FlowCleanup import OK')"` → exit code 0. Or remove the Smoke subsection if no distinct smoke check exists (and remove `smoke` from frontmatter `verify`).

findings.issue: Required Skills section is in Russian ('Перед выполнением задачи загрузи:') while all other tasks in the batch use English for this section. Post-completion is also in Russian (original template language). Inconsistent with the feature's task style.
findings.fix: Change to English: 'Before executing this task, load:'. Update Post-completion items to English as in tasks 1-4.

findings.issue: `test_no_flows_retained_after_100_requests` verifies `flow count == 0` which is a structural/count assertion. Tech-spec Testing Strategy integration test specifies 'process 100 flows, verify no flow objects retained (assert flow count == 0)' — the test matches, but the TDD Anchor description only says 'flow count is 0' without mentioning that this tests memory management (no unbounded retention). Weak description obscures the purpose.
findings.fix: Add purpose to description: 'after 100 proxied requests, zero flow objects are held in master state — confirms FlowCleanup + minimal Master prevents unbounded flow retention'

decomposition_checks.wave_ordering: correct — tasks 1,2 in wave 1; tasks 3,4 in wave 2 (depend on wave 1); task 5 in wave 3 (depends on wave 2)
decomposition_checks.merge_candidates: none — each task has a distinct responsibility and modifies distinct files
decomposition_checks.split_candidates: none — task 3 touches 2 files (main.py + proxy_addon.py) for a single cohesive feature (RAM monitoring with idle guard requiring _last_flow_time in addon); within acceptable scope
decomposition_checks.traceability: tasks 1-5 map 1:1 to tech-spec Implementation Tasks 1-5; file lists match
decomposition_checks.shared_resources: mitmproxy Master owned by Task 1 (_start_mitmproxy); Task 3 (RAM restart) and Task 5 (integration tests) consume it; both have depends_on on Task 1 (directly or transitively via depends_on:[3,4] → 3 depends on [1,2]). Compliant.
